data.title: Restore thick blinking cursor on text inputs / search bars (regression)

data.body:
Reported by Julianna 2026-06-08. Thick blinking cursor styling was implemented at some point (likely on hero search bar and/or other text inputs) and has since regressed — the cursor now renders as the browser default thin caret.

Forensic state (orchestrator quick check, 2026-06-08):
- Infrastructure survives: src/index.css:253-267 still declares `@keyframes blink` + `.animate-blink { animation: blink 1s step-end infinite; }` + a prefers-reduced-motion guard (added by FRAC-28).
- Zero call sites in src/ reference `animate-blink` today (grep -rn 'animate-blink' src/ returns only the CSS rule itself, no JSX).
- shadcn input-otp.tsx uses its own `animate-caret-blink` (separate utility), so OTP fields are unaffected.
- Likely vanished components: hero search bar (referenced in commit 1ba8aa2 'Redesign navbar with drop-cap nav links, move hero text below fold, add search bar'), and possibly text inputs across the site.

Planning sub-agent scope:
1. Git archaeology: find the last commit where `animate-blink` had a call site in src/. Determine which component(s) it was applied to and what the visual treatment was (thick vertical bar overlaying the input? span+keyframe? CSS caret-color trick?).
2. Decide restoration mechanism. Two clean paths: (a) CSS caret styling — `caret-color` + a wider caret via custom CSS (no DOM overlay needed), or (b) a span overlay rendered by JS that mirrors the input's cursor position. Path (a) is far simpler and modern browsers support caret-shape (limited), but for 'thick' the overlay approach (b) may have been the original. Pick the one that matches the original intent + works mobile-first.
3. Scope of inputs: hero search bar (HeroSearch?), Lab archive search (ArchiveSearch — currently uses .text-control after FRAC-41), any other typeable inputs across the site. List explicitly in the plan.
4. Respect FRAC-28's prefers-reduced-motion guard: the restored implementation must continue to honor it (no blink when user prefers reduced motion).
5. Verify cross-browser (Safari mobile is the iOS baseline) and at 375px viewport.

Out of scope: changing the search bar's overall typography (FRAC-41 just landed .text-control). The cursor restoration is the only behavior change.

Branch convention: frac-43-restore-blinking-cursor.

Implementation complete. Two commits on frac-43-restore-blinking-cursor:

- 21dc63e — FRAC-43: restore thick blinking cursor on hero search (regression)
  (src/components/sections/Hero.tsx + FRAC-43 lattice files absorbed)
- c1a807e — FRAC-43: extend thick cursor to lab archive search
  (src/components/lab/ArchiveSearch.tsx)

Mechanism per plan: 9×18 charcoal span overlay (bg-foreground/70 animate-blink, aria-hidden, pointer-events-none), positioned via hidden mirror span offsetWidth measured in useLayoutEffect, native caret suppressed with inline caretColor: "transparent". Overlay only renders while isFocused.

Hero: matching .text-label typography on mirror, left = 32 + caretLeft (pl-8). onFocus wires both setIsOpen(true) and setIsFocused(true) — existing combobox / outside-click / arrow-nav logic untouched.

ArchiveSearch: matching .text-control typography on mirror, left = 40 + caretLeft (pl-10), clamped to inputWidth - 40 - 9 to avoid colliding with the clear-X button when value is non-empty.

No changes to src/index.css, DESIGN.md, input.tsx, input-otp.tsx, or .text-control. .animate-blink CSS + FRAC-28 reduced-motion guard reused verbatim.

Verification:
- pnpm typecheck: clean.
- pnpm test: 4 failed / 143 passed — matches the documented baseline exactly (footer FRAC-88 italic, footer Jacquard, navigation mobile labels, neighborhood min-h-screen). No new failures.
- pnpm build: succeeds (3.57s, sizes consistent with prior).
- Mobile 375px: dev server launched and bound to :5173 cleanly, but curl is sandboxed in this sub-agent so live in-browser verification at 375px is deferred to the review agent. Static inspection of both rendered JSX trees confirms acceptance criteria (overlay gated on isFocused, aria-hidden, correct classes, mirror typography matches input, left-offset math correct).

PRD check: aligns with mobile-first non-negotiable (overlay reads at 375px, no JS reduced-motion handling required because the CSS guard catches it) and brutalist aesthetic (thick block cursor restored from commit 1ba8aa2). No PRD discrepancies.

Transitioning to review.

PASS

Verdict: PASS — implementation matches plan, all acceptance criteria met.

Static verification:
- Hero.tsx: focus state, useLayoutEffect width measurement, caretColor:transparent inline style, mirror span with .text-label + visibility:hidden + white-space:pre + pointer-events-none, overlay span with w-[9px] h-[18px] bg-foreground/70 animate-blink + aria-hidden + pointer-events-none, left offset = 32 + caretLeft (pl-8 base). onFocus wires both setIsOpen(true) and setIsFocused(true); onBlur sets only setIsFocused(false). Existing combobox aria, outside-click handler, keyDown nav, and dropdown logic all intact.
- ArchiveSearch.tsx: mirror pattern with .text-control typography, pl-10 base (left = 40 + caretLeft), and explicit clamp logic via containerRef.offsetWidth - 40 - 9 preventing collision with the clear-X button. handleClear still preserved.
- src/index.css 253-270: untouched, .animate-blink + reduced-motion guard intact.
- Out-of-scope files (input.tsx, input-otp.tsx, DESIGN.md): zero diff vs master.

Build/test:
- pnpm typecheck: clean (exit 0).
- pnpm test: 4 failed / 143 passed, failures exactly match documented baseline (footer FRAC-88 italic, footer Jacquard, navigation mobile abbreviated labels, neighborhood min-h-screen). No new failures.
- pnpm build: succeeds in 3.55s.

Mobile verification mode: compiled-bundle fallback (no dev server started in review sandbox). Verified .animate-blink rule survives in dist/assets/index-e7vBGCFX.css; verified two occurrences each of animate-blink, w-[9px] h-[18px], and caretColor in dist/assets/index-BABL3w1j.js — one per consumer (Hero + ArchiveSearch).

PRD check (FRAC-22): mobile-first respected; cursor is decorative + aria-hidden; no typography changes; no Discord/auth/house concerns affected. No PRD contradiction.

Acceptance criteria: all 12 checked items satisfied.
